issues: Ticker bar in the header feels slightly cramped with too many coins — spacing between items could breathe more
The candlestick chart overlaid on the area chart creates visual noise — mixing chart types in one panel without clear differentiation makes data harder to parse
Mini sparklines in the KPI cards are extremely subtle and low contrast, barely visible against dark card backgrounds
Holdings table is cut off at the bottom — only one row (BTC) is fully visible, undermining data completeness in the viewport
Watchlist section on the right is very narrow and only partially visible — DOT entry is cut off, making it feel incomplete
No visible sidebar navigation — all navigation is top-bar only, which works but limits scalability perception for a 'vault' product
The green accent (#00e676 or similar) occasionally reads as too bright/saturated on the chart fill, verging on neon for a professional finance app
Portfolio value in the nav ($111,023.81) and the KPI card ($124,839) show different numbers without explanation — could cause user confusion
Card borders are very subtle to the point of being nearly invisible — a slightly more defined border would improve card separation
Time period selector (7D/1M/3M/1Y) uses a teal filled button for active state which is good, but inactive states have low contrast against the dark background
strengths: Excellent use of a dark, professional crypto-native color palette — deep navy/slate backgrounds feel premium and domain-appropriate
Live ticker tape in the header is a standout feature that immediately communicates the product's real-time nature
KPI cards with embedded sparklines are a nice polish touch — well-proportioned and clean
Portfolio Performance chart has genuine data with realistic candlestick patterns layered over an area chart — data completeness is strong
Green accent color is highly appropriate for a finance/crypto dashboard (gains, growth)
Holdings table has proper column headers (ASSET, PRICE, 24H CHANGE, HOLDINGS, VALUE) with realistic formatting
The coin color indicators (yellow dot for BTC) in the holdings table add a professional touch
Overall layout density is well-calibrated for a trading dashboard — information-rich without feeling overwhelming
The portfolio value badge in the header (PORTFOLIO $111,023.81 +4.32%) is a sophisticated UX pattern borrowed from professional trading platforms
Depth and layering between the header, KPI row, chart panel, and bottom tables is clearly communicated
specific_improvements: Reconcile the portfolio value discrepancy: the header shows $111,023.81 while the KPI card shows $124,839 — either make them match or add a label like 'Invested' vs 'Total Value' to explain the difference
Reduce the green area chart fill opacity from ~30% to ~15% and desaturate slightly to keep it professional rather than neon — reference: Bloomberg Terminal or Robinhood's subtle fills
Add 4-5 more rows to the Holdings table (ETH, SOL, BNB, ADA visible in the ticker) so the table section looks populated in the initial viewport
Increase the sparkline stroke weight in KPI cards from ~1px to 1.5-2px and boost opacity to 70% for better legibility at small sizes
Add a left sidebar or collapsible nav panel with icons for Dashboard, Portfolio, Markets, Trade, Settings — the current header-only nav limits the 'vault' product feel
Separate the candlestick chart from the area chart visually — either use a toggle to switch between 'Price' and 'Portfolio' views, or use a secondary Y-axis with a clear legend
Expand the Watchlist panel to show at least 4-5 full entries with sparklines, 24h change %, and market cap — currently it feels underdeveloped compared to the Holdings section
Add a subtle gradient border (1px, using the green accent at 30% opacity) around the active KPI card or chart panel to create a focal point
Apply tabular-nums font variant to all numeric displays to prevent layout shift and improve alignment in the table columns
Consider adding a 'Last updated' timestamp or a live pulse indicator near the chart title to reinforce the real-time data aspect of the product
